name: Embalse de Gata Loop from Moraleja
name_original: Villasbuenas de Gata desde Moraleja – circular del Embalse de Gata
description: This moderate 27.3-mile road cycling route circles the Embalse de Gata, offering scenic views and passing through traditional villages in Si
region_intro: Road cycling around Moraleja, located in the Cáceres province of Extremadura, Spain, offers diverse terrain for cyclists. The region is characterized by picturesque river valleys, notably the Rivera de Gata, and significant water bodies like the Borbollón Reservoir. Cyclists encounter varied landscapes, from rolling hills to more challenging ascents in areas bordering the Sierra de Gata mountains. The routes primarily feature paved surfaces, providing suitable conditions for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Moraleja The most popular road cycling route is Embalse de Gata Loop from Moraleja, a 27.3 miles (44.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route circles the Embalse de Gata, offering scenic views of the reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Coria from Moraleja – circular through Montehermoso and the Presa del Embalse de Borbollón, a moderate 56.3 miles (90.7 km) path. This route traverses varied landscapes, including the Presa del Embalse de Borbollón. Local road cyclists also love the Penha Garcia from Moraleja – circular through the Reserva Natural de Sierra de Malcata, a 77.9 miles (125.4 km) trail leading through the Reserva Natural de Sierra de Malcata, often completed in about 5 hours 48 minutes. Road cycling around Moraleja is defined by the Rivera de Gata, the Borbollón Reservoir, and the foothills of the Sierra de Gata mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to difficult. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 225 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Moraleja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Moraleja?
A: Road cycling around Moraleja offers a diverse landscape. You'll encounter picturesque river valleys, particularly along the Rivera de Gata, and significant water bodies like the Borbollón Reservoir. Routes range from rolling hills to more challenging ascents in areas bordering the Sierra de Gata mountains. The surfaces are primarily paved, making them ideal for road cycling.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Moraleja area?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes available around Moraleja, catering to various skill levels. These include 2 easy routes, 7 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes near Moraleja?
A: Yes, Moraleja offers several excellent circular road cycling routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1057801400/embalse-de-gata-loop-from-moraleja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Gata Loop from Moraleja&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 44 km trail offering scenic views of the reservoir. Another option is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056354990/coria-from-moraleja-circular-through-montehermoso-and-the-presa-del-embalse-de-borbollon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coria from Moraleja – circular through Montehermoso and the Presa del Embalse de Borbollón&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 90.7 km and traverses varied landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056339398/penha-garcia-from-moraleja-circular-through-the-reserva-natural-de-sierra-de-malcata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penha Garcia from Moraleja – circular through the Reserva Natural de Sierra de Malcata&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding option. This difficult route stretches over 125 km with significant elevation gain, leading through the Reserva Natural de Sierra de Malcata.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes feature the picturesque Rivera de Gata, offering beautiful riverside landscapes. You can also cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3333558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borbollón Dam&lt;/a&gt;, a significant water body. The region also borders the Sierra de Gata mountains, known for their tranquil natural surroundings. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5992396" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piscina Natural La Alameda&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5995492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path to the Mirador del Boquerón and Laguna de los Jornus&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, you can incorporate cultural stops into your rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6047850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge of Coria&lt;/a&gt;, with Roman origins, is a notable historical site. Charming villages like Villasbuenas de Gata, with its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5690813" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Village Square&lt;/a&gt;, and La Moheda de Gata, featuring its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3333532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Square and Church&lt;/a&gt;, also offer interesting stops.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Moraleja?
A: The region around Moraleja, particularly the Sierra de Gata mountains, benefits from a mild microclimate. This suggests that spring and autumn would be ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures. Summer might be warm, while winter could still be enjoyable due to the mild conditions.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Moraleja?
A: Road cyclists frequently praise the varied terrain and scenic beauty of Moraleja. The region's mix of river valleys, reservoirs, and mountain foothills provides engaging routes for all levels. The komoot community has actively explored the area, with over 225 road cyclists using the platform to discover its diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed ride?
A: Yes, Moraleja offers easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a more relaxed pace. While specific easy routes are not detailed here, the overall network includes options suitable for less experienced riders, allowing them to enjoy the scenery without excessive challenge.

Q: Can I find routes that follow the Rivera de Gata?
A: Absolutely. The Rivera de Gata is a prominent natural feature in the region, and several routes incorporate its scenic beauty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Along the Rivera de Gata – Cilleros Village Center loop from Moraleja&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 61 km route that allows you to experience the picturesque riverside landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through traditional villages?
A: Yes, you can cycle through charming traditional villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24530456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoyos – Historic Center of Hoyos loop from Moraleja&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 43.8 km route that takes you through Hoyos, a village known for its stately atmosphere and traditional architecture in the Sierra de Gata mountains.
tips: Public mud bath with curative powers next to the Ribera de Gata reservoir, 4 km from Villasbuenas de Gata.

The town's main square is known as Plaza del Palacio (Palace Square), and it serves as the administrative center and one of the town's most iconic landmarks. It is where the Villasbuenas de Gata Town Hall is located.

Situated in the heart of the town center, it is a popular meeting place for locals.

Moraleja is a regional town located in the Sierra de Gata between the Sierra de los Ángeles and the Pico de la Almenara, on the banks of the Árrago River. In summer the river beach is full of life and activities.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the town of Moraleja, which is the starting point for this circular route. Look for designated parking areas near the town center, especially around the Moraleja Town Center and Stone Bridge.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Gata region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions in spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures, making the moderate climbs more enjoyable and the scenery vibrant. Summers can be quite hot, so if cycling then, an early morning start is recommended.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It follows public roads and paths around the Embalse de Gata and through the Sierra de Gata region.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a 'moderate bike ride'. While it's primarily on paved surfaces, the distance of nearly 44 km and an elevation gain of around 360 meters mean it requires a good fitness level. Beginners might find it challenging, but those with some road cycling experience should enjoy it.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is designed for racebikes, meaning it primarily features paved surfaces suitable for road cycling. You'll experience rolling hills and some moderate climbs as you circle the Embalse de Gata, offering a smooth ride through the natural landscape.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: As you cycle, you'll enjoy picturesque views of the Embalse de Gata. Notable points of interest include the La Cochina Bath, the charming Villasbuenas de Gata Village Square and Church, and the historic Moraleja Town Center and Stone Bridge.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other notable trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, a small section (approximately 1%) of this route overlaps with the historic Cañada Real de Gata. This ancient livestock trail offers a glimpse into the region's traditional pathways, though the majority of your ride will be on paved roads.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Given that this is a racebike route primarily on paved roads, it is generally not ideal for dogs. While there are no specific restrictions, the nature of road cycling makes it less suitable for canine companions compared to off-road trails.

faqs.question: What kind of natural scenery can I expect?
faqs.answer: You can expect beautiful natural scenery, especially around the Embalse de Gata. The route passes through landscapes rich in Mediterranean flora, including holm oak and cork oak woodlands, which provide a habitat for local wildlife. The tranquil atmosphere of the Sierra de Gata mountains is a constant companion.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Moraleja and Villasbuenas de Gata, the towns you'll pass through, offer opportunities for refreshments. You can find cafes and pubs in their respective town centers to refuel during your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: For this moderate road cycling route, we recommend bringing your racebike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, water bottles, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and sun protection. Don't forget your phone for navigation and emergencies.
